message.content:
#set page(margin: 1in)
#set text(font: "New Computer Modern", size: 11pt)

#align(center, [
  #text(size: 1.4em, weight: "bold")[Inline and Display Mathematics]
  #v(0.5em)
  Source-backed grouped formula sample
])

= Expressions

*Expression 1.* The following expression is taken from a source-backed formula corpus.

$ 1 / sqrt(-g') partial_mu (sqrt(-g') g'^(mu nu) partial_nu phi) + 1 / chi [ (d V_1) / (d phi) - 1 / chi (d V_2) / (d phi) + alpha / M_p P(phi) e^(alpha phi / M_p) ] = - (h gamma) / (sqrt(2) M_p) overline(Psi)' Psi' phi (e^(gamma phi / M_p)) / (chi^(3/2)) $

*Expression 2.* The following expression is taken from a source-backed formula corpus.

$ (psi_1_((alpha_1, alpha_2, beta_3 + s_3)) product_(j=3)^(p-2) psi_1_((beta_j + s_j, alpha_j, beta_(j+1) + s_(j+1))) psi_1_((alpha_(p-1), beta_(p-1) + s_(p-1), alpha_p))) / (psi_1_((alpha_1, alpha_2, beta_3)) product_(j=3)^(p-2) psi_1_((beta_j, alpha_j, beta_(j+1))) psi_1_((alpha_(p-1), beta_(p-1), alpha_p))) = psi_(0,p) [alpha, beta, s] $

*Expression 3.* The following expression is taken from a source-backed formula corpus.

$ e^(i hat(Gamma) [vec(phi)_1, vec(phi)_2]) = angle T_C "exp" [ i / delta integral d^4 x sum_(j=1)^2 { (-)^(j-1) cal(L)' (vec(phi)_j (x) + vec(phi)_j (x)) - (delta hat(Gamma) [vec(phi)_1, vec(phi)_2]) / (delta vec(phi)_j (x)) dot vec(phi)_j (x) } ] angle $

*Expression 4.* The following expression is taken from a source-backed formula corpus.

$ Q_+ = integral d x { overline(psi)_-^(overline(j)) (x) ( (partial) / (partial overline(Phi)^(overline(j))) (x) + 1/2 G_(i overline(j)) (partial Phi^i (x)) / (partial x) ) + psi_+^j (x) Omega_j (Phi(x)) - psi_+^l overline(psi)_-^(overline(i)) overline(psi)_+^(overline(j)) (partial G_(l overline(j))) / (partial overline(Phi)^(overline(i))) } $

*Expression 5.* The following expression is taken from a source-backed formula corpus.

$ (D_-^(alpha, alpha', beta, beta', gamma) t^(-rho)) (x) = (Gamma(beta' + rho) Gamma(-alpha - alpha' + gamma + rho) Gamma(-alpha' - beta + gamma + rho)) / (Gamma(rho) Gamma(-alpha' + beta' + rho) Gamma(-alpha - alpha' - beta + gamma + rho)) x^(alpha + alpha' - gamma - rho) $
